fields.narrative_intro: *A slumped person and a mischievous person discuss vibe-coding gold rushes at a robot rights protest outside Congress.*
fields.panel1:
[setup]
SLUMPED PERSON: "I built an app on Replit. Passive income, baby."
fields.panel2:
[build]
MISCHIEVOUS PERSON: "Nice! How much did you make in 24 days?"
SLUMPED PERSON: "Technically I made zero. I *spent* $1,982."
fields.panel3:
[pivot]
MISCHIEVOUS PERSON: "Token costs are impossible to predict upfront."
SLUMPED PERSON: "That's what the influencer forgot to mention."
fields.panel4:
[silent reaction]
[Visual: The slumped person holds up a phone showing a TikTok: 'I make 10k a month from home thanks to Replit!!' — the mischievous person slowly turns to face the camera with dead eyes, holding a protest sign that now reads 'RIGHTS FOR MY REMAINING SAVINGS'.]
fields.panel5:
[punch]
SLUMPED PERSON: "At least Herbalife gave me a free shaker cup."
MISCHIEVOUS PERSON: "AI gave you a 404 and a billing alert."
fields.panel6:
[button]
[Visual: A passing robot carrying a 'ROBOTS DESERVE DIGNITY' sign glances at the phone bill, shudders, and quickens its pace.]

narrative_summary: A broke 22-year-old who burned $1,982 on Replit tokens in 24 days realizes vibe-coding's MLM grift is even worse than Herbalife — at least Herbalife had physical product — while a protest robot wants no part of this economy.
rationale: Candidate 3 earns its win on punchline and fit: 'At least Herbalife gave me a free shaker cup / AI gave you a 404 and a billing alert' is a genuinely earned comparison that advances the Herbalife thesis with a specific, unexpected detail rather than just restating it. The TikTok visual in panel 4 also does real comedic work, grounding the MLM-influencer angle in a concrete, recognizable format.
story_title: Generative AI Is Having Its Herbalife Moment
place: at a robot rights protest outside Congress
hat_pair: jester hat with three drooping bells
